pi_items.source: export_result.sh and verifier.py output path checks
verifier.py expects max_x - min_x to be 60.0
verifier.py expects max_y - min_y to be 40.0
verifier.py EXPECTED_POINTS contains inward points at x=5.0 and x=55.0, bounded by width 60.0
verifier.py EXPECTED_POINTS slot heights are bounded by y=15.0 and y=25.0
verifier.py EXPECTED_POINTS starts slot at y=15.0 from the base
verifier.py EXPECTED_POINTS notch is defined between x=15.0 and x=45.0 (width 30.0)
verifier.py EXPECTED_POINTS slopes from (15,40) to (30,25) and (30,25) to (45,40) have perpendicular slopes of -1 and 1
verifier.py tolerance checks: abs(ex - nx) <= 0.5 and abs(ey - ny) <= 0.5

privileged_info_summary: The task requires generating a new 2D sketch from a blank canvas in SolveSpace without any pre-existing data. Verification confirmed the agent must construct a fully-constrained 15-point polygon mimicking a machining V-block and save it to '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/v_block_profile.slvs'. Expected bounds match a 60.0 mm width and 40.0 mm height. The verifier precisely checks coordinates that enforce two rectangular clamping slots with a 5.0 mm depth, 10.0 mm height, positioned 15.0 mm from the base. The top V-notch is evaluated for a width of 30.0 mm matching an orthogonal (90-degree) 15.0 mm depth profile, within an allowable tolerance of 0.5 mm for all geometric checks.
